mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth expands corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in GDP growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, elevated borrowing costs compress equity valuations and reduce forward S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in the unemployment rate and declines in total nonfarm payrolls indicate labor market deterioration, which reduces consumer spending and equity forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not decline when UNRATE rises and PAYEMS falls concurrently over a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals robust manufacturing output and supply chain stability, boosting S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days specifically during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to produce positive 5-day forward returns during identified recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.4 million units signals weakening construction demand and broader economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive when HOUST drops below 1400 for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with restrictive monetary policy erodes real corporate margins and suppresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns do not decline when CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS both remain elevated simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and food services revenue indicate strong consumer demand, which directly supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth shows no predictive power for 20-day forward returns in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals labor market cooling, which reduces consumer spending and equity forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are not significantly negative when UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points within a quarter.
